User.title: User
User.description: Represents a general user within the ScholarlyFlow system, acting as a base for specific roles like Student, Teacher, or Parent. Authentication details are managed externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, typically used for login and notifications.

role.description: The assigned role of the user within the system (e.g., 'Admin', 'Teacher', 'Student', 'Parent').

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student enrolled in the school, linked to a general user account and containing academic-specific information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

userId.description: Reference to the associated User entity for general user information. (Relationship: User 1:1 Student)

studentIdNumber.description: A unique identifier assigned to the student within the school's administrative system.

enrollmentDate.description: The date when the student was first enrolled in the school.

gradeLevel.description: The current academic grade level or year of the student (e.g., '10th Grade', 'Freshman').

parentIds.description: References to Parent entities associated with this student. (Relationship: Parent N:N Student)

classIds.description: References to Class entities in which the student is currently enrolled. (Relationship: Class N:N Student)

Teacher.title: Teacher
Teacher.description: Represents a teacher employed by the school, linked to a general user account and containing professional information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Teacher entity.

userId.description: Reference to the associated User entity for general user information. (Relationship: User 1:1 Teacher)

teacherIdNumber.description: A unique identifier assigned to the teacher within the school's administrative system.

department.description: The academic department or faculty the teacher belongs to.

hireDate.description: The date when the teacher was hired by the school.

qualifications.description: A list of the teacher's academic qualifications, degrees, and certifications.

subjectIds.description: References to Subject entities that the teacher is qualified and assigned to teach. (Relationship: Teacher N:N Subject)

classIds.description: References to Class entities that the teacher is currently instructing. (Relationship: Teacher N:N Class)

Parent.title: Parent
Parent.description: Represents a parent or legal guardian of one or more students, linked to a general user account.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Parent entity.

userId.description: Reference to the associated User entity for general user information. (Relationship: User 1:1 Parent)

studentIds.description: References to Student entities who are children of this parent. (Relationship: Parent N:N Student)

Subject.title: Subject
Subject.description: Represents an academic subject offered within the school curriculum.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subject entity.

name.description: The full name of the academic subject (e.g., 'Mathematics', 'World History').

code.description: A short, unique alphanumeric code for the subject (e.g., 'MATH101', 'HIST202').

description.description: A brief description outlining the content and objectives of the subject.

Class.title: Class
Class.description: Represents a specific offering or section of a subject, including its assigned teacher, schedule, and enrolled students.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Class entity.

name.description: The specific name of the class section (e.g., 'Algebra I - Section A', 'Biology II - Lab').

subjectId.description: Reference to the Subject entity that this class teaches. (Relationship: Subject 1:N Class)

teacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher entity assigned to instruct this class. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Class)

academicYear.description: The academic year during which this class is offered (e.g., '2023-2024').

schedule.description: Details of the class schedule (e.g., 'Mon, Wed, Fri 9:00 AM - 10:00 AM').

roomNumber.description: The physical room number where the class is regularly held.

studentIds.description: References to Student entities currently enrolled in this class. (Relationship: Class N:N Student)

Assignment.title: Assignment
Assignment.description: Represents an academic assignment or task given to students within a specific class.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Assignment entity.

title.description: The title or name of the assignment.

description.description: A detailed description of the assignment, including instructions and requirements.

classId.description: Reference to the Class entity to which this assignment belongs. (Relationship: Class 1:N Assignment)

subjectId.description: Reference to the Subject entity this assignment is related to. (Relationship: Subject 1:N Assignment)

teacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher entity who created this assignment. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Assignment)

dueDate.description: The date and time by which the assignment is expected to be submitted.

maxPoints.description: The maximum possible points a student can earn for this assignment.

assignmentType.description: The category or type of the assignment (e.g., 'Homework', 'Quiz', 'Project', 'Essay').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the assignment was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the assignment was last updated.

Submission.title: Submission
Submission.description: Represents a student's submission for a particular assignment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Submission entity.

assignmentId.description: Reference to the Assignment entity this submission is for. (Relationship: Assignment 1:N Submission)

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity who made this submission. (Relationship: Student 1:N Submission)

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the assignment was submitted.

submissionContentUri.description: A URI or path pointing to the actual submitted content (e.g., a file, a document link).

gradeId.description: Reference to the Grade entity associated with this submission, if it has been graded. (Relationship: Grade 1:1 Submission)

Grade.title: Grade
Grade.description: Represents the score and qualitative feedback given to a student for a specific assignment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Grade entity.

assignmentId.description: Reference to the Assignment entity this grade is associated with. (Relationship: Assignment 1:N Grade)

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity who received this grade. (Relationship: Student 1:N Grade)

score.description: The numerical score obtained by the student for the assignment.

feedback.description: Qualitative feedback or comments provided by the teacher regarding the student's performance.

gradedByTeacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher entity who performed the grading. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Grade)

gradedDate.description: The date and time when the assignment was graded.

gradeLetter.description: The letter grade equivalent (e.g., 'A', 'B+', 'C') derived from the score, if applicable.

Attendance.title: Attendance
Attendance.description: Records the attendance status of a student for a specific class session or date.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Attendance entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity whose attendance is being recorded. (Relationship: Student 1:N Attendance)

classId.description: Reference to the Class entity for which this attendance record applies. (Relationship: Class 1:N Attendance)

date.description: The specific date for which the attendance is recorded.

status.description: The attendance status for the student on the given date (e.g., 'Present', 'Absent', 'Late', 'Excused Absent').

recordedByTeacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher entity who recorded this attendance. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Attendance)

notes.description: Any additional notes or remarks regarding the attendance record.

Exam.title: Exam
Exam.description: Represents an exam or test administered to students in a particular class, providing details about its structure and schedule.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Exam entity.

title.description: The title or name of the exam.

description.description: A detailed description of the exam, including topics covered and format.

classId.description: Reference to the Class entity for which this exam is being conducted. (Relationship: Class 1:N Exam)

subjectId.description: Reference to the Subject entity this exam covers. (Relationship: Subject 1:N Exam)

teacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher entity who created and will administer the exam. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Exam)

examDate.description: The scheduled date and time for the exam.

durationMinutes.description: The planned duration of the exam in minutes.

maxPoints.description: The maximum possible points achievable on the exam.

examType.description: The category or type of exam (e.g., 'Midterm', 'Final', 'Quiz').

ExamResult.title: ExamResult
ExamResult.description: Records a student's score and feedback for a specific exam.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExamResult entity.

examId.description: Reference to the Exam entity this result is for. (Relationship: Exam 1:N ExamResult)

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity who took the exam. (Relationship: Student 1:N ExamResult)

score.description: The numerical score obtained by the student on the exam.

feedback.description: Qualitative feedback or comments provided by the teacher for the exam.

gradedByTeacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher entity who graded the exam. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N ExamResult)

gradedDate.description: The date and time when the exam was graded.

Fee.title: Fee
Fee.description: Represents a financial fee or charge associated with a student, managed by the school.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Fee entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity to whom this fee applies. (Relationship: Student 1:N Fee)

description.description: A description of what the fee is for (e.g., 'Tuition Fee', 'Library Fine', 'Activity Fee').

amount.description: The total amount of the fee.

dueDate.description: The date by which the fee is due for payment.

status.description: The current payment status of the fee (e.g., 'Outstanding', 'Paid', 'Overdue', 'Waived').

paidDate.description: The date and time when the fee was paid, if applicable.

paymentMethod.description: The method used for payment (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'Bank Transfer', 'Cash').

transactionId.description: An identifier for the payment transaction, typically from an external payment system.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification message or alert sent to a user within the application, visible on their dashboard.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

recipientUserId.description: Reference to the User entity who is the intended recipient of this notification. (Relationship: User 1:N Notification)

senderUserId.description: Reference to the User entity who initiated or generated this notification (e.g., a teacher, admin). (Relationship: User 1:N Notification)

message.description: The content or body of the notification message.

type.description: The category or type of the notification (e.g., 'assignment_due', 'grade_posted', 'announcement', 'fee_reminder').

read.description: A boolean flag indicating whether the recipient has viewed or read the notification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the notification was created.

relatedEntityId.description: Optional: Reference to an entity related to the notification (e.g., Assignment ID, Class ID) for deep linking.

definition.description: Stores general user information for authentication and core profile details. The document ID (`userId`) maps directly to Firebase Authentication UIDs (`request.auth.uid`). Includes 'role' for role-based access control.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores student-specific academic information. The document ID (`studentId`) maps to the 'id' field of the Student entity. Includes 'userId' for direct linkage to the general user account, 'parentIds' and 'classIds' for relationship-based access.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Student entity.

definition.description: Stores teacher-specific professional information. The document ID (`teacherId`) maps to the 'id' field of the Teacher entity. Includes 'userId' for direct linkage to the general user account, 'subjectIds' and 'classIds' for relationships.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Teacher entity.

definition.description: Stores parent-specific information. The document ID (`parentId`) maps to the 'id' field of the Parent entity. Includes 'userId' for direct linkage to the general user account and 'studentIds' for relationship-based access.

params.name: parentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Parent entity.

definition.description: Stores details about academic subjects offered in the school. Accessible globally for listing and reference.

params.name: subjectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Subject entity.

definition.description: Stores details about a specific class offering. Includes denormalized 'teacherId' and 'studentIds' array for authorization independence, allowing direct checks in security rules for class members and teachers.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Class entity.

definition.description: Stores academic assignments related to a specific class. Includes denormalized 'classId', 'subjectId', and 'teacherId' for authorization independence, enabling direct rule checks without parent document lookups.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Class.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Assignment entity.

definition.description: Stores student submissions for a specific assignment. Includes denormalized 'assignmentId' and 'studentId' for authorization independence, simplifying rules for submitters and teachers.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Class.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Assignment.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Submission entity.

definition.description: Stores grades for student assignments. Includes denormalized 'assignmentId', 'studentId', and 'gradedByTeacherId' for authorization independence, allowing students, parents, and teachers to access relevant grades directly.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Class.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Assignment.

params.name: gradeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Grade entity.

definition.description: Records attendance status for students in a top-level collection. Includes denormalized 'studentId' and 'recordedByTeacherId' for role-based authorization queries.

params.name: attendanceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Attendance entity.

definition.description: Represents an exam or test for a specific class. Includes denormalized 'classId', 'subjectId', and 'teacherId' for authorization independence, simplifying access rules for class members and teachers.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Class.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Exam entity.

definition.description: Records a student's score and feedback for an exam. Includes denormalized 'examId', 'studentId', and 'gradedByTeacherId' for authorization independence, allowing direct rule checks for students, parents, and teachers.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Class.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Exam.

params.name: examResultId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ExamResult entity.

definition.description: Manages financial fees in a top-level collection. Each fee contains a 'studentId' to allow for role-based queries. Admins can access all fees, while students can query for their own.

params.name: feeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Fee entity.

definition.description: Stores notification messages and alerts for a specific user. Includes denormalized 'recipientUserId' for authorization independence, ensuring only the intended recipient can read their notifications.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the recipient User.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Notification entity.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore data structure for ScholarlyFlow emphasizes a secure, scalable, and debuggable design by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy employed is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization**. This is crucial for simplifying security rules and enabling atomic operations.

Here's how Authorization Independence is achieved:

1.  **User-Centric Denormalization**: For entities that represent specific roles (Student, Teacher, Parent), their corresponding `userId` is explicitly stored within their document (e.g., `Student.userId`, `Teacher.userId`, `Parent.userId`). This allows rules to directly verify `request.auth.uid` against the document's `userId` for self-management, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to a parent `users` collection to infer a role or ownership.

2.  **Parent Context in Subcollections**: For subcollections where access depends on attributes of a logical parent, those attributes are denormalized into the subcollection's documents. For example:
    *   `Class` documents include `teacherId` and an array of `studentIds`. This allows rules for `Assignments`, `Exams`, `Submissions`, and `Grades` to check if `request.auth.uid` matches the `teacherId` or is present in the `studentIds` array of the *current* `Class` document or any related subdocument directly, without needing to fetch the parent `Class` document via `get()`.
    *   `Assignment` documents include `classId` and `teacherId`. Similarly, `Exam` documents include `classId` and `teacherId`.
    *   `Submission` documents include `assignmentId` and `studentId`.
    *   `Grade` and `ExamResult` documents include `assignmentId`/`examId`, `studentId`, and `gradedByTeacherId`.
    *   `Attendance` and `Fee` documents, now in top-level collections, include `studentId` and relevant teacher/parent UIDs to enable secure, direct queries without subcollection constraints.
    *   `Notification` documents include `recipientUserId`.
    This denormalization ensures that all necessary authorization context is present within the document being accessed, making rules atomic, faster, and easier to debug.

**QAPs (Query-Authorization Patterns) Support:**

1.  **Structural Segregation**: Data with different security postures is separated into distinct collections. For instance, `/users/{userId}` stores general user data, while `/students/{studentId}`, `/teachers/{teacherId}`, and `/parents/{parentId}` store role-specific profiles. `Subjects` are globally readable, while `Classes` are more restricted.

2.  **Path-Based Ownership for Private Data**: Personal user data (e.g., `/users/{userId}/notifications/{notificationId}`) is structured under the user's ID, making it straightforward to implement rules like `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`.

3.  **Efficient List Queries**: Due to denormalization, `list` operations (QAPs) are highly efficient and secure:
    *   A student can query their grades by filtering `grades` documents where `studentId == request.auth.uid` (e.g., across all assignments in their enrolled classes, or within a specific assignment subcollection).
    *   A teacher can query assignments for their classes by filtering `assignments` documents where `teacherId == request.auth.uid`.
    *   A parent can query their child's attendance by filtering `attendance` documents where their child's `studentId` is present.
    This structure allows client-side queries to include the necessary filters, and server-side rules to enforce those filters based on the denormalized fields, preventing unauthorized data access during list operations without complex `get()` calls for each document.

4.  **Role Management (DBAC)**: The `User` entity includes a `role` field (`Admin`, `Teacher`, `Student`, `Parent`). This allows for simple, direct rule checks like `request.resource.data.role == 'Admin'` or `get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == 'Admin'`. For more fine-grained role-based access to specific resources (e.g., a teacher accessing their assigned class's data), the `teacherId` denormalized within `Class` documents and its subcollections (Assignments, Exams, etc.) serves as the access control mechanism.

This comprehensive approach minimizes security rule complexity, enhances debugging, and ensures a scalable and performant Firestore backend.
